_note: Original Practice questions aligned with CS Navigator's original Learn lesson for this topic.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 1
questions.prompt: Which modifier limits a field to its own class?
choices: private
public
package
import
questions.explanation: A private member can be accessed only inside the class that declares it. Callers must use the public operations the class provides.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 2
questions.prompt: Which line imports ArrayList from its package?
choices: import java.util.ArrayList;
package java.util.ArrayList;
include java.util.ArrayList;
using java.util.ArrayList;
questions.explanation: Java import statements begin with `import` and use the full package and class name. The statement ends with a semicolon.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers summary
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Organize classes and control which code may use their details.
It is used only to print a message.
It automatically fixes every program error.
It removes the need for variables and tests.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. Use `public` for code meant for callers, `private` for class details, and default access for code shared only inside the package.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers good practice
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Group classes that solve related problems.
Skip input checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide every error and continue without checking the result.
Put all of the work in one long statement.
questions.explanation: Group classes that solve related problems. This keeps the code easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 5
questions.prompt: Which version follows the lesson's safer approach?
choices: public int credits;
Both versions are equally safe.
Neither version can run.
questions.explanation: A private field prevents outside code from placing the object in an invalid state. A method can check the new value before saving it.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 6
questions.prompt: Which statement best explains 'Folders and package names must agree'?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
If a file declares `package school.records`, keep it under the matching `school/records` path for normal builds. A mismatch often leads to class loading or import errors.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: If a file declares `package school.records`, keep it under the matching `school/records` path for normal builds. A mismatch often leads to class loading or import errors.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 7
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Match package names to the directory structure.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Match package names to the directory structure. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 8
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
Keep fields private unless outside access is truly required.
questions.explanation: Keep fields private unless outside access is truly required. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 9
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Expose small public methods with clear jobs.
Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Expose small public methods with clear jobs. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 10
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Import classes instead of repeating long names throughout a file.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Import classes instead of repeating long names throughout a file. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.
